audit_items.proof_trace: `ber_strdup()` at L71 in `ldif_open_url` can return NULL on allocation failure, but the result `p` is passed without any NULL check to `ldap_pvt_hex_unescape(p)` at L80, which dereferences `*s` immediately at url.c:L1625 — causing a NULL pointer dereference crash. All other `ber_strdup` callers in libldap (ldif.c:L113, ldifutil.c:L637) do NULL-check the return value, confirming this is a coding defect, not intentional design.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker induces an OOM condition while an LDIF file containing an `include: file://path` directive is processed (e.g., via slapd config reload or ldapadd with crafted input); `ber_strdup` returns NULL, and the subsequent `ldap_pvt_hex_unescape(NULL)` at fetch.c:L80 dereferences NULL at url.c:L1625, crashing the LDAP server (DoS).
